sections.heading: Cluster Tagging Considerations
sections.body:
Before you begin, review
the [requirements](resource-tag-params), [considerations](resource-tag-considerations), [best practices](tag-best-practices), [prerequisites](tag-prerequisites), and
[suggested tags](suggested-tags).

sections.heading: Manage Cluster Tags from the Clusters Page
sections.body:
You can add a tag, view existing tags, and manage tags from
the **Clusters** page. To learn more, see
[performance metrics](monitor-cluster-metrics).

sections.heading: Step 1: Manage tags.
sections.body:
- In the lower-left corner of the cluster,
  view any existing tags.

- On the cluster, click **Add Tag**
  or **Manage tags**.

  > **Important:** Don't include sensitive information such as Personally Identifiable
  > Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in your
  > resource tags. Other MongoDB services, such as Billing, can access
  > resource tags. MongoDB also sends cluster resource tags to DataDog
  > and Prometheus integrations. Resource tags are *not* intended for
  > private and sensitive data. To learn more, see
  > [sensitive information](sensitive-info).

- To manage your tags, see [Manage Tags](manage-tags-database).

- Click **Save**.

sections.heading: Manage cluster Tags from the Overview Page
sections.body:
You can add a tag, view existing tags, and manage tags from
the **Overview** page. To learn more, see
[performance metrics](monitor-cluster-metrics).

sections.heading: Step 1: Go to the  page for your project.
sections.body:
- Click the name of the cluster to open the **Cluster** sidebar.

- Click **Overview**.

sections.heading: Step 2: Manage tags.
sections.body:
- In the upper-left corner of the **Overview** page,
  view any existing tags.

- In the **Tags** section, click **Add Tag** or
  **Manage tags**.

  > **Important:** Don't include sensitive information such as Personally Identifiable
  > Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in your
  > resource tags. Other MongoDB services, such as Billing, can access
  > resource tags. MongoDB also sends cluster resource tags to DataDog
  > and Prometheus integrations. Resource tags are *not* intended for
  > private and sensitive data. To learn more, see
  > [sensitive information](sensitive-info).

- To manage your tags, see [Manage Tags](manage-tags-database).

- Click **Save**.

sections.heading: Apply a cluster Tag to a New Cluster from a Template
sections.body:
You can add a tag, view existing tags, and manage tags when you create
a new cluster from a template. To learn more, see
[Create a Cluster](create-new-cluster).

sections.heading: Step 1: Specify a tag key and value.
sections.body:
- Click **Build a Database**.

- In the **Tag** section, specify a **Key** and
  **Value**.

  > **Important:** Don't include sensitive information such as Personally Identifiable
  > Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in your
  > resource tags. Other MongoDB services, such as Billing, can access
  > resource tags. MongoDB also sends cluster resource tags to DataDog
  > and Prometheus integrations. Resource tags are *not* intended for
  > private and sensitive data. To learn more, see
  > [sensitive information](sensitive-info).

sections.heading: Apply cluster Tags to a New Cluster with Advanced Options
sections.body:
You can add a tag, view existing tags, and manage tags when you create
a new cluster. To learn more, see
[Create a Cluster](create-new-cluster).

sections.heading: Step 1: Open the  Dialog.
sections.body:
- If you already have one or more clusters, click
  **Create** to display the
  **Create New Cluster** dialog box.

- If this is your first cluster:

  - Click **Build a Database**.

  - Click **advanced configuration options** at the
    top of the screen to display the
    **Create New Cluster** dialog box.

sections.heading: Step 2: Manage tags.
sections.body:
- In the **Cluster Details**, click
  **Add Tag**.

  > **Important:** Don't include sensitive information such as Personally Identifiable
  > Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in your
  > resource tags. Other MongoDB services, such as Billing, can access
  > resource tags. MongoDB also sends cluster resource tags to DataDog
  > and Prometheus integrations. Resource tags are *not* intended for
  > private and sensitive data. To learn more, see
  > [sensitive information](sensitive-info).

- To manage your tags, see [Manage Tags](manage-tags-database).

sections.heading: Manage cluster Tags When You Modify an Existing Cluster
sections.body:
You can add a tag, view existing tags, and manage tags when you modify
an existing cluster. To learn more, see [modify all configuration options](scale-cluster)
or [Manage Flex Clusters in MongoDB Atlas](manage-flex-clusters).

sections.heading: Step 1: Go to the cluster settings.
sections.body: Click **Edit Config** and select your cluster in the drop-down menu.

sections.heading: Step 2: Manage tags.
sections.body:
- In the **Cluster Details**, click
  **Add Tag**.

  > **Important:** Don't include sensitive information such as Personally Identifiable
  > Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in your
  > resource tags. Other MongoDB services, such as Billing, can access
  > resource tags. MongoDB also sends cluster resource tags to DataDog
  > and Prometheus integrations. Resource tags are *not* intended for
  > private and sensitive data. To learn more, see
  > [sensitive information](sensitive-info).

- To manage your tags, see [Manage Tags](manage-tags-database).

sections.heading: Step 1
sections.body:
Navigate to your resource tags using one of the procedures
described on this page.

sections.heading: Step 2: Manage the tag.
sections.body:
> **Important:** Don't include sensitive information such as Personally Identifiable
> Information (PII) or Protected Health Information (PHI) in your
> resource tags. Other MongoDB services, such as Billing, can access
> resource tags. MongoDB also sends cluster resource tags to DataDog
> and Prometheus integrations. Resource tags are *not* intended for
> private and sensitive data. To learn more, see
> [sensitive information](sensitive-info).

Do any of the following tasks:

- To add a tag, click Add tag, and specify a
  Key and Value.

- To edit a tag, update the Key or Value
  for the tag.

- To delete a tag, click the 
  Delete icon next to the tag.

  > **Note:** If you delete a tag, Atlas removes the tag from the
  > associated resources.
